page.content: The first table lists the sources of heat along with their estimated heat load and heat sink location, including Atomic oven with an estimated heat load of 20–30 W located at 300 K outside dilution refrigerator, DC wires with 3 mW located at multiple stages, coax cables with 5 mW also at multiple stages, laser beams with 1.8 mW at the 4 K stage, and RF dielectric dissipation with 5 mW at the 100 mK stage. The second table presents the composition of various lignin types with their respective percentages of Carbon (C), Hydrogen (H), Nitrogen (N), and Degree of Unsaturation. Kraft lignin has 62.2% C, 6.1% H, 0.9% N, and a degree of unsaturation of 3. The [Py][Ac]-K. lignin contains 63.7% C, 5.8% H, 1.4% N, and also a degree of unsaturation of 3. [Mim][Ac]-K. lignin holds 63.5% C, 6.0% H, 3.7% N, and a degree of unsaturation of 3.

page.content: The [Pyrr][Ac]-K. lignin has 64.5% C, 7.2% H, 4.0% N, and a degree of unsaturation of 3, while the [Pyrr][Ac]-CS lignin shows 52.1% C, 10.3% H, 10.1% N, and a degree of unsaturation of 1. Finally, EF-CS contains 43.0% C, 5.7% H, 0.9% N, and a degree of unsaturation of 2.
